Soroban no_std header.description: no_std crate header with the minimal soroban_sdk imports. Soroban contracts must be no_std because the WASM runtime does not provide std.

Soroban contract skeleton.description: Minimal #[contract] struct plus #[contractimpl] block. This is the canonical Soroban contract entry point.

Soroban contract test module.description: Wires up a sibling test.rs module so unit tests are compiled out of the deployed WASM.

body: Address, Bytes, BytesN, Env, IntoVal, Map, String, Symbol, Vec,
Soroban prelude imports.description: Common multi-import covering most Soroban contract use cases: macros, host types, and helpers.

Soroban single import.description: Empty soroban_sdk use statement, ready to fill in with the symbols you need.

Soroban contracttype enum.description: Storage-key enum decorated with #[contracttype] so it can cross the host boundary.

Soroban contracttype struct.description: Host-compatible struct: #[contracttype] makes it serialisable into Soroban storage and arguments.

body: #[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, PartialOrd, Ord)]
Soroban contracterror enum.description: Typed contract-error enum. Variants are encoded as u32 status codes that clients can decode.

Soroban contractmeta macro.description: Embeds metadata (description, version, repo URL, etc.) into the compiled WASM customsection.

Soroban contract trait.description: Trait + #[contractimpl] pair. Useful for SEP-41 / token-style interfaces where you want a shared trait surface.

Soroban symbol_short macro.description: Compile-time short Symbol (≤9 chars). Cheaper than runtime Symbol::new for storage keys and event topics.

Soroban Symbol::new.description: Runtime Symbol for names longer than 9 chars. Use symbol_short! when possible — it is free at runtime.

body: pub fn initialize(env: Env, ${1:admin}: Address) -> Result<(), ${2:Error}> {
Soroban initialize function.description: One-shot initializer that refuses to run twice. Always store an Admin and extend TTL on the instance entry.

Soroban public contract method.description: Skeleton of a public contract function. The first parameter must be `env: Env` so the body has host access.

body: pub fn ${1:transfer}(env: Env, ${2:from}: Address, ${3:to}: Address, ${4:amount}: i128) -> Result<(), ${5:Error}> {
Soroban Result-returning fn.description: Function returning a typed Result. Prefer this over panicking — clients get a structured error.

Soroban storage getter.description: Persistent storage getter with a sane default. Avoids panics on first-time reads.

Soroban storage setter (with TTL bump).description: Persistent setter that extends the entry's TTL right after writing. Prevents premature archival.

Soroban admin-only function.description: Admin-gated state mutation. `require_auth` enforces that the current admin signed the invocation.

Soroban pausable pattern.description: Pausable contract pattern: an admin-controlled boolean and a guard helper that mutating functions must call first.

body: pub fn upgrade(env: Env, new_wasm_hash: BytesN<32>) {
Soroban upgrade WASM.description: Standard admin-gated WASM upgrade. Always require_auth — without it anyone can swap your bytecode.

Soroban storage: instance get.description: Read from instance storage (cleared with the contract). Cheap to extend, lifetime tied to contract instance.

Soroban storage: instance set.description: Write to instance storage and extend its TTL. Instance storage shares a single TTL for the whole contract.

Soroban storage: persistent get.description: Read a per-key persistent entry. Each key has its own TTL — be sure to bump it when you read or write.

Soroban storage: persistent set.description: Write to persistent storage and extend its TTL right after. Skipping the bump risks early archival.

Soroban storage: temporary get.description: Read from temporary storage. Cheaper than persistent; entries are unrecoverable once expired.

Soroban storage: temporary set.description: Write to temporary storage and extend TTL. Use for rate limits, nonces, or anything ephemeral.

Soroban storage: extend TTL.description: Extend an entry's TTL. Picks between persistent / temporary / instance via a multiple-choice tab stop.

Soroban storage: has key.description: Existence check that does not deserialise the value. Cheaper than `get(...).is_some()`.

Soroban auth: require_auth.description: Asserts that the address has authorised the current invocation. Required before any sensitive state change.

Soroban auth: require_auth_for_args.description: Authorises a specific argument tuple. Lets callers approve narrow operations rather than blanket invocation auth.

Soroban auth: admin check helper.description: Reusable admin-gate helper. Call at the top of any admin-only function.

Soroban auth: contract auth context.description: Iterate over the current auth contexts. Useful for assertions in tests and for advanced multi-auth flows.

Soroban token: client.description: Construct a token client against a SAC or SEP-41 contract address.

Soroban token: transfer.description: Transfer tokens between two addresses. The `from` address must `require_auth` somewhere up the call stack.

Soroban token: mint.description: Mint tokens via the StellarAssetClient surface. Requires the token admin to authorise.

Soroban token: burn.description: Burn tokens from `from`. The address must require_auth.

Soroban token: balance.description: Read a token balance for the given owner address.

Soroban event: publish single topic.description: Publish an event with a single short symbol topic. Topics are how indexers and clients filter events.

Soroban event: multiple topics.description: Multi-topic event (e.g. transfer events emit `(transfer, from, to)` topics with amount as data).

Soroban log macro.description: Diagnostic log emitted to host output. Stripped from optimised release builds — fine to leave in code.

Soroban contract client.description: Generated client for a deployed contract. Macro-generated by #[contractimpl].

Soroban cross-contract invoke.description: Low-level cross-contract call. Prefer the generated client when you control both contracts; this helps for dynamic calls.

Soroban current contract address.description: Returns the address of the currently executing contract. Useful for self-references in events and storage.

Soroban deploy from contract.description: Deploy a child contract using the current contract as factory. The salt determines the resulting address.

Soroban ledger timestamp.description: Deterministic ledger close-time in unix seconds. Use this instead of std::time inside contracts.

Soroban ledger sequence.description: Current ledger sequence number. Common as a randomness commit, deadline ledger, or schedule key.

Soroban ledger network id.description: Network passphrase hash. Useful for cross-network signature scoping.

Soroban panic_with_error.description: Panic with a typed contract error so callers see a structured failure instead of an opaque host error.

Soroban error conversion.description: Implement `From<OtherError>` so the `?` operator can bubble nested errors up into your contract error enum.

Soroban return Result with ?.description: Bubble up a Result error using `?`. Pair with a `From` impl that targets your contract error enum.

Soroban test module.description: Test module skeleton with Env, mocked auths, and a registered contract client.

Soroban Env::default.description: Spin up a test Env and mock every auth so calls do not need explicit signers.

Soroban register contract & client.description: Register a contract under test and grab its generated client. Pair with Env::default.

Soroban mock specific auths.description: Tighter alternative to mock_all_auths: assert that a specific address authorised a specific call.

Soroban budget profile.description: Reset and print the resource budget around a contract call. Useful for catching gas regressions in tests.

Soroban DataKey enum pattern.description: Convention DataKey enum covering admin, pause flag, balance map, and allowance map.

Soroban Vec literal.description: Construct a Soroban Vec literal with the env-bound `vec!` macro.

Soroban Map::new.description: Construct a host-compatible Map. Use this instead of HashMap inside contracts.

Soroban BytesN<32>.description: Fixed-length 32-byte buffer. Used for WASM hashes, ledger network IDs, and Ed25519 keys.

Soroban storage TTL constants.description: Common TTL constants in ledgers (~5 second close time → 17,280 ledgers per day). Use as `extend_ttl(THRESHOLD, EXTEND_TO)`.
